key_points: Er:YAG laser radiation delivered through a flexible waveguide achieves antibacterial effect in root canal walls and surrounding tissues.
Therapeutic doses of Er:YAG laser radiation enable one-step disinfection of root canals, including elimination of anaerobic microorganisms.
The use of a flexible waveguide allows delivery of Er:YAG radiation into curved root canals, extending the clinical applicability of this laser in endodontics.
Calcium hydroxide is mentioned in the context of endodontic treatment alongside laser disinfection.

outcomes.measured: Antibacterial effect in root canal walls and surrounding tissues
outcomes.result: Er:YAG laser via flexible waveguide achieved disinfection including anaerobic microorganisms
outcomes.clinical_significance: One-step disinfection of root canals potentially reducing treatment sessions

notes: Full text not available; extraction based on abstract only. Study type is unclear from abstract — may be a clinical case series or in vitro/ex vivo study. Specific laser parameters (power, fluence, pulse duration, frequency) not reported in the abstract.

population.condition: Endodontic infection requiring root canal disinfection

source_citation: Dostálová, T., Jelínková, H., Houšová, D., Šulc, J., Němeć, M., Dušková, J., Miyagi, M., & Krátký, M. (2002). Endodontic Treatment with Application of Er:YAG Laser Waveguide Radiation Disinfection. Journal of Clinical Laser Medicine & Surgery. https://doi.org/10.1089/104454702760090218
target_tissue: dentin, root canal walls, periapical tissue

drugs_mentioned.name: calcium hydroxide
drugs_mentioned.dental_relevance: Mentioned in the context of endodontic treatment alongside Er:YAG laser disinfection
